symbols.name: \(\kappa\)
symbols.def: Lower bound on every active latent-state mass.

symbols.name: \(\eta\)
symbols.def: Lower bound on the second singular value of each conditional proxy measurement operator.

symbols.name: \(y_{am}(r)\)
symbols.def: Outcome response under treatment \(a\) and mediator \(m\).

symbols.name: \(g\)
symbols.def: Argument of a conditional proxy measurement operator.

symbols.name: \(\sigma_2(B)\)
symbols.def: Second largest singular value of a real matrix \(B\).

symbols.name: \(\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}\)
symbols.def: Informatively incomplete proxy-factorized simple-mediation model from \(def:model-class\).

symbols.name: \(P^\dagger\)
symbols.def: Rational positive-cell observed law sought by \(oeq:robust-strict-contraction\).

symbols.name: \(d_\infty(P,P^\dagger)\)
symbols.def: Maximum absolute difference between corresponding treatment-conditional observed cells.

novel.justification: A finite latent sensitivity analysis fixes substantively present risk strata. A positive mass floor rules out padding a selected three-state resolution with unused labels.
Two pre-treatment measurements collected through administratively distinct channels can have conditionally independent measurement errors given a shared latent risk state.

assumptions.condition: \(q^Z_{zu}=q^Z_{zu'}\) and \(q^W_{wu}=q^W_{wu'}\) for every \(z\in\mathcal Z\), \(w\in\mathcal W\), and \(u,u'\in\mathcal U_3\) satisfying \(c(u)=c(u')\).

novel.justification: Administrative and laboratory proxies can be calibrated to a recorded two-tier risk label while a third latent state captures within-tier clinical severity. Distinct collection channels retain conditional independence given the full risk state, but neither channel records the within-tier distinction.
The proxy may fail completeness because one latent direction is missing while its two observed directions remain separated by a prespecified measurement-quality threshold.
This condition excludes a nearly constant outcome proxy while allowing the single missing third direction that creates conditional completeness failure.
The joint proxy table has two stable measurement directions, so nonidentification is attributable to an explicit third-direction failure rather than a vanishing proxy signal.

definitions.name: \(c\)
definitions.construction: \(c:\mathcal U_3\to\mathcal C\) is given by \(c(1)=c(2)=0\) and \(c(3)=1\).

definitions.name: \(\mathsf C^Z_{a,m},\mathsf C^W_{a,m},\mathsf C^{ZW}_{a,m}\)
definitions.construction: \([\mathsf C^Z_{a,m}(\lambda,q^Z)g](z)=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U_3}g(u)q^Z_{zu}\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r)\}\), \([\mathsf C^W_{a,m}(\lambda,q^W)g](w)=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U_3}g(u)q^W_{wu}\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r)\}\), and \([\mathsf C^{ZW}_{a,m}(\lambda,q^Z,q^W)g](z,w)=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U_3}g(u)q^Z_{zu}q^W_{wu}\sum_{r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r)\}\) for every \(g\in\mathbb R^3\), \(a\in\{0,1\}\), and \(m\in\mathcal M\).

definitions.name: \(\mathcal F_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\)
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal F_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)=\{(\lambda,q^Z,q^W):\lambda_{ur}\ge0,\sum_{u\in\mathcal U_3,r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}=1,q^Z_{zu}\ge0,\sum_{z\in\mathcal Z}q^Z_{zu}=1,q^W_{wu}\ge0,\sum_{w\in\mathcal W}q^W_{wu}=1,\pi_u(\lambda)\ge\kappa\ \forall u\in\mathcal U_3,q^Z_{zu}=q^Z_{zu'}\text{ and }q^W_{wu}=q^W_{wu'}\ \forall(z,w,u,u')\text{ with }c(u)=c(u'),\sigma_2\{\mathsf C^Z_{a,m}\}\ge\eta,\sigma_2\{\mathsf C^W_{a,m}\}\ge\eta,\sigma_2\{\mathsf C^{ZW}_{a,m}\}\ge\eta\ \forall(a,m)\in\{0,1\}\times\mathcal M,p_{azwmy}=\sum_{u\in\mathcal U_3,r\in\mathcal R}\lambda_{ur}q^Z_{zu}q^W_{wu}\mathbf 1\{m=m_a(r),y=y_{a,m_a(r)}(r)\}\ \forall(a,z,w,m,y)\}\), where \(\mathsf C^Z_{a,m}=\mathsf C^Z_{a,m}(\lambda,q^Z)\), \(\mathsf C^W_{a,m}=\mathsf C^W_{a,m}(\lambda,q^W)\), and \(\mathsf C^{ZW}_{a,m}=\mathsf C^{ZW}_{a,m}(\lambda,q^Z,q^W)\).

definitions.name: \(\mathscr H(P)\)
definitions.construction: For rational \(P\in\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\), form a polynomial sign description of the constraints in \(def:proxy-feasible-set\), using principal-minor descriptions for the three \(\sigma_2\)-inequalities and the equalities induced by \(c\); append either \(\psi(\lambda)=L_0(P)\) or \(\psi(\lambda)=U_0(P)\), and apply cylindrical algebraic decomposition with Positivstellensatz dual recovery to return quantifier-free sign systems excluding the appended endpoint face.

definitions.name: \(\mathscr H_{\mathrm{rw}}\)
definitions.construction: Augment the response type in \(def:response-alphabet\) by finite potential responses for an observed treatment-induced mediator--outcome confounder, retain its shared values across nested assignments, impose the proxy-factorization and shared-tier coarsening constraints, and run the endpoint-face elimination in \(def:endpoint-handle\) on the resulting nested path-specific mean.

statements.statement: For every \(P^\star\in\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}\) and every \((a,m)\in\{0,1\}\times\mathcal M\), \(\operatorname{rank}\{\mathsf C^Z_{a,m}\}=\operatorname{rank}\{\mathsf C^W_{a,m}\}=\operatorname{rank}\{\mathsf C^{ZW}_{a,m}\}=2<3\).
statements.justification: The common coarsened tier makes latent states one and two observationally indistinguishable to every proxy view, while separation retains two measurement directions.
statements.gap: Completeness failure is usually imposed as an operator rank restriction. This lemma derives it from the shared-tier measurement mechanism used by the proposal.
statements.consumer: It verifies that the strict-contraction question lies outside every conditional bridge-injectivity route specified by the model.

statements.statement: For every \(P\in\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\), the identified set of \(\tau^{\mathrm{ND}}=E_{P^\star}[Y(1,M(0))-Y(0,M(0))]\) over \(\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\) equals \(\Theta_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\). Its endpoints are \(L_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\) and \(U_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\), and both are attained by members of \(\mathfrak M_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\).
statements.justification: The finite program keeps the cross-world coupling of \(M(0)\) while enforcing the selected shared-tier proxy measurement structure and the full observed-law marginal, including treatment assignment.
statements.gap: DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 supplies the general discrete SCM optimization template. This is a model-specific exact representation, not a claimed generic advance over that template.
statements.consumer: It makes the proxy-refined natural-direct-effect endpoints observed-data computable on the explicitly feasible domain \(\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\).

statements.statement: For every \(P\in\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\), \(\Theta_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\subseteq\Theta_0(P)\), and hence \(L_0(P)\le L_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\le U_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)\le U_0(P)\).
statements.justification: Marginalizing any proxy-factorized completion leaves a valid no-proxy response-type completion of the observed mediation margins.
statements.gap: Sjolander2009 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 give no-proxy natural-effect floors without negative-control factorization. The containment is supporting machinery needed to formulate a strict tightening.
statements.consumer: It certifies that endpoint exclusion in the main question is a valid contraction of the Sjölander--Breum no-proxy interval.

statements.statement: After deleting the proxy-factorization and conditional measurement restrictions from \(def:proxy-feasible-set\) and taking \(\mathcal Z\) and \(\mathcal W\) to be singleton sets, its projection onto response-type masses is exactly \(\mathcal F_0(P)\).
statements.justification: The construction has the standard no-information limit.
statements.gap: This is a required reduction check rather than a claimed novel result.
statements.consumer: It connects the proposed endpoints to the established no-proxy natural-direct-effect analysis.

statements.statement: Construct rational \(P^\dagger\in\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\), \(\epsilon>0\), and an endpoint-exclusion sign system in \(\mathscr H(P^\dagger)\) such that \(\mathcal N_\epsilon(P^\dagger)\subseteq\mathcal D_{\kappa,\eta}^+\) and either \(L_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)>L_0(P)\) for every \(P\in\mathcal N_\epsilon(P^\dagger)\) or \(U_{3,\kappa,\eta}^{\mathrm{inc}}(P)<U_0(P)\) for every \(P\in\mathcal N_\epsilon(P^\dagger)\).
statements.justification: A rational, positive-cell certificate with an open neighborhood would show that incomplete but informative two-tier proxy measurements have content beyond a reparameterized no-proxy mediation model.
statements.gap: BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 and Sjolander2009 provide no-proxy natural-effect bounds, whereas DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 point identifies natural effects through bridge conditions. No verified result gives a factorization-constrained, failed-completeness endpoint-exclusion certificate for this observed-mediator target.
statements.consumer: The certificate provides a finite sensitivity-analysis regime in which proxy data demonstrably shorten a published no-proxy natural-direct-effect floor.

statements.statement: For a finite mediation extension containing an observed recanting witness, can the response-type augmentation in \(\mathscr H_{\mathrm{rw}}\) produce an analogous positive-cell endpoint-exclusion certificate under the shared proxy coarsening and separation conditions?
statements.justification: This isolates the downstream obstacle created by shared treatment-induced-confounder responses without displacing the simple-mediation natural direct effect from the headline.
statements.gap: BaiWuSunCui2026 and WuBaiCui2026 obtain point identification for recanting-witness targets through proximal strategies; neither characterizes the factorization-constrained sharp set after the relevant completeness failures.
statements.consumer: A resolution transfers the verified simple-mediation certificate architecture to path-specific analyses with recanting witnesses.

sampling_model.units: Each unit has latent state \(U\) and response type \(R\).
sampling_model.treatment: \(A\) is randomized with positive assignment probabilities.
sampling_model.overlap: The certificate domain requires every treatment-conditional \((Z,W,M,Y)\) cell to have positive probability.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: This is a population partial-identification proposal; endpoint estimation and inference are outside scope.

tldr: The headline is the simple-mediation natural direct effect. In a fixed three-state latent model with positive active masses, factorized binary negative-control proxies that measure a shared two-tier risk label rather than within-tier severity, and stable two-direction conditional measurement, the project asks for a rational positive-cell observed law whose exact proxy-factorized response-function interval strictly contracts the Sjölander--Breum no-proxy interval on an open observed-data neighborhood. The shared coarsening makes completeness fail for every proxy view without imposing a nongeneric joint-rank equality. The generic polynomial program is supporting machinery. A nested recanting-witness effect is delegated to a downstream open-ended extension.

project_justification.gap: Completeness-based proximal mediation identifies natural effects only when the relevant conditional proxy operators are injective, whereas no-proxy natural-effect bounds discard proxy factorization. The literature has no observed-data endpoint-exclusion certificate for a three-state risk model in which two proxy channels stably measure a common coarsened risk tier but cannot distinguish within-tier severity.
project_justification.niche: The model preserves the cross-world coupling of \(Y(1,M(0))\), fixes an active three-state sensitivity resolution, and makes failed completeness arise from a substantive shared measurement coarsening rather than an isolated rank equality.
project_justification.fill: The project supplies an exact factorization-constrained feasible set and seeks a rational positive-cell strict contraction with a semialgebraic certificate that persists over an observed-data neighborhood.

related_work: Sjolander2009 gives foundational no-proxy bounds for natural direct effects under randomized treatment and confounded intermediate variables. BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 derives sharp no-proxy natural and separable mediation bounds under unmeasured intermediate-variable confounding; Open-ended Question 1 seeks a strict tightening of this no-proxy floor within the stated finite proxy-factorized model. DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 gives three proximal point-identification strategies for natural direct and indirect effects under bridge conditions, while the shared-tier proxy measurement structure here makes all conditional proxy views noninjective despite stable two-direction measurement. DorettiStanghellini2024 gives binary natural-effect intervals under no unobserved exposure--mediator confounding and partially constant cross-world dependence. Its PC-CWD model neither contains nor is contained in the present model: it does not impose a finite latent proxy factorization, while the present response-type model does not impose PC-CWD. ZhangSu2024 obtains valid proxy bounds from partial knowledge of \(P(W\mid U)\) without completeness. That partial-transition-information model neither contains nor is contained in the present one, which instead fixes two conditionally independent proxy channels that share a coarsened measurement tier and targets a cross-world observed-mediator natural direct effect. LuoGeng2016 gives direct and indirect effect bounds for a continuous endpoint with unobserved mediator--outcome confounding. HanLuoZhangGu2026 tightens controlled-direct-effect bounds when part of that confounder is observed; both are adjacent mediation-bound results, not targets of the proposed strict tightening. BaiWuSunCui2026 identifies recanting-witness path-specific effects using proximal bridges and motivates only the downstream extension. WuBaiCui2026 treats hidden recanting witnesses with proximal identification and semiparametric inference, not a failed-completeness sharp set. ZhangShanLi2026 point identifies interventional effects with proxies for unmeasured treatment-induced confounding, which is a different estimand from the natural direct effect. GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023 obtains no-completeness proxy bounds for treatment effects and analogous hidden-mediator settings, including cross-world hidden-mediator contrasts, but it does not impose the observed-mediator response-type coupling and proxy-factorization program used here. DuarteFinkelsteinKnoxMummoloShpitser2024 provides the discrete SCM polynomial-optimization machinery used for the supporting representation.
honest_scope: All claims are conditional on the fixed three-state, positive-mass, proxy-factorized model with a shared two-tier proxy measurement coarsening and separated two-direction conditional measurement. They do not claim unrestricted-cardinality sharpness, point identification, inference, PC-CWD robustness, partial transition-matrix knowledge, or a reduction of the natural direct effect to GhassamiShpitserTchetgen2023. The strict-contraction witness and its semialgebraic neighborhood certificate remain open. If the proxy restrictions are removed and the proxies are singleton, the program reduces to the standard no-proxy response-function set.

bibliography.citation: Arvid Sjölander (2009), "Bounds on Natural Direct Effects in the Presence of Confounded Intermediate Variables," \emph{Statistics in Medicine} 28, 558--571.
Alexander Balke and Judea Pearl (1997), "Bounds on Treatment Effects from Studies with Imperfect Compliance," \emph{Journal of the American Statistical Association} 92, 1171--1176.
Marie S. Breum, Vanessa Didelez, Erin E. Gabriel, and Michael C. Sachs (2025), "Bounds for Causal Mediation Effects," \emph{arXiv:2512.11549}.
Marco Doretti and Elena Stanghellini (2024), "Interval Identification of Natural Effects in the Presence of Outcome-Related Unmeasured Confounding," \emph{arXiv:2405.13621}.

Zhiheng Zhang and Xinyan Su (2024), "Partial Identification with Proxy of Latent Confoundings via Sum-of-ratios Fractional Programming," \emph{Proceedings of the Fortieth Conference on Uncertainty in Artificial Intelligence}, PMLR 244, 4140--4172.
Peng Luo and Zhi Geng (2016), "Bounds on Direct and Indirect Effects of Treatment on a Continuous Endpoint," \emph{ACM Transactions on Intelligent Systems and Technology} 7, Article 20.

Yu Han, Peng Luo, Wei Zhang, and Xiang Gu (2026), "Bounds and Identification on Direct and Indirect Effects under Partially Observed Mediator-Endpoint Confounders," \emph{Journal of Multivariate Analysis} 213, 105565.
Oliver Dukes, Ilya Shpitser, and Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023), "Proximal Mediation Analysis," \emph{Biometrika} 110, 973--987.
Yang Bai, Sihan Wu, Baoluo Sun, and Yifan Cui (2026), "Proximal Path-Specific Inference," \emph{arXiv:2605.09462}.
Sihan Wu, Yang Bai, and Yifan Cui (2026), "Proximal Mediation Analysis with Hidden Recanting Witnesses," \emph{arXiv:2606.17600}.
Xiaoying Zhang, Jiawei Shan, and Wei Li (2026), "Proximal Mediation Analysis with Unmeasured Treatment-Induced Confounding," \emph{arXiv:2607.02901}.
AmirEmad Ghassami, Ilya Shpitser, and Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023), "Partial Identification of Causal Effects Using Proxy Variables," \emph{arXiv:2304.04374}.

Guilherme Duarte, Noam Finkelstein, Dean Knox, Jonathan Mummolo, and Ilya Shpitser (2024), "An Automated Approach to Causal Inference in Discrete Settings," \emph{Journal of the American Statistical Association} 119, 1778--1793.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: Bounds are derived for natural direct effects with randomized treatment and a confounded intermediate variable.
Sharp no-proxy bounds are derived for natural and separable mediation effects under unmeasured intermediate-variable confounding.
Binary natural-effect intervals are derived under no unobserved exposure--mediator confounding and partially constant cross-world dependence.
Valid proxy bounds are computed without completeness using partial knowledge of the proxy transition matrix.
Bounds for direct and indirect effects are derived for a continuous endpoint with unobserved mediator--outcome confounding.
Controlled-direct-effect bounds are tightened when part of the mediator--endpoint confounder is observed.
Three bridge-based strategies point identify natural direct and indirect effects under proximal assumptions.
Bridge-based strategies identify a recanting-witness path-specific effect.
Proximal strategies identify path-specific effects with hidden recanting witnesses.
Four proximal identification results are given for interventional effects with unmeasured treatment-induced confounding.
No-completeness proxy bounds are derived for treatment effects and related hidden-mediator settings.
Finite discrete SCM queries admit constrained response-function optimization representations.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: The core makes a robust positive-cell strict contraction of a no-proxy natural-direct-effect floor, rather than a generic response-function representation, the focal object; the nested recanting-witness target is only a downstream question.
novelty_justification.published_axis: DukesShpitserTchetgen2023 supplies completeness-based point identification, while Sjolander2009 and BreumDidelezGabrielSachs2025 supply no-proxy natural-effect bounds. DorettiStanghellini2024 imposes PC-CWD and ZhangSu2024 assumes partial proxy-transition knowledge, neither of which nests the shared-tier proxy-factorized model. The open object is a semialgebraically certified strict contraction under that conditionally noncomplete model; the program itself is only supporting machinery.

literature_checklist.author: Sjölander
literature_checklist.one_line: Foundational no-proxy bounds define the natural-direct-effect floor targeted by the strict contraction.

literature_checklist.author: Breum et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Sharp no-proxy natural and separable mediation bounds are the immediate comparator floor.

literature_checklist.author: Dukes, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
literature_checklist.one_line: Bridge-based proximal point identification of natural effects delimits the completeness boundary.

literature_checklist.author: Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
literature_checklist.one_line: No-completeness proxy bounds, including hidden-mediator settings, distinguish the present observed-mediator response-type target.

literature_checklist.author: Doretti and Stanghellini
literature_checklist.one_line: Binary natural-effect intervals under PC-CWD form a non-nested cross-world mediation-bound comparator.

literature_checklist.author: Zhang and Su
literature_checklist.one_line: Proxy partial identification from partial transition-matrix knowledge is non-nested with the shared-tier factorization model.

literature_checklist.author: Luo and Geng
literature_checklist.venue: ACM Transactions on Intelligent Systems and Technology
literature_checklist.one_line: Continuous-endpoint mediation bounds are an adjacent non-proxy comparator.

literature_checklist.author: Han et al.
literature_checklist.one_line: Partially observed mediator--endpoint confounders sharpen controlled-direct-effect bounds in an adjacent setting.

literature_checklist.author: Wu, Bai, and Cui
literature_checklist.one_line: Hidden recanting-witness proximal identification motivates the delegated downstream extension.

literature_checklist.author: Duarte et al.
literature_checklist.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
literature_checklist.one_line: Discrete SCM optimization supplies the supporting exact-program method.
